methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Alpine Eagle, a Seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Jan-Hendrik Boelens and Timo Breuer, is evaluated against a peer set comprising 4 private companies at the same or adjacent stage (Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, and Tenna Systems) alongside a broader universe of 8 private peers and 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS).

The peer-median approach, the primary applicable method given available data, yields a $225M valuation based on the median across the 4-company peer set, which ranges from $60M (Tenna Systems, Seed) to $500M (Stark Defence, Series A). This method reflects peer valuations at equivalent or slightly advanced stages within the same sector. The triangulated analysis, constrained by single-method applicability, establishes a valuation range of $191M to $259M, centered on the $225M median.

The substantial gap between Alpine Eagle's €10.25M raised and the implied $225M valuation warrants scrutiny. Key uncertainties include: the relevance of Series A comps (Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries) to a Seed-stage asset; the degree to which frontier defensibility, team, or product-market fit premium justifies the peer median; and whether the peer set adequately reflects market conditions and stage-specific risk. The analysis supports a valuation range of $191M–$259M, though confidence is limited to the comparability of the reference universe and the applicability of peer multiples to an early-stage entity.
